reasoning: This 24-bit ADC with 256 kSPS sampling rate does not meet any CCL control thresholds. ECCN 3A001.a.5 controls ADCs but only when specific resolution/sampling rate combinations are exceeded. The lowest threshold is 16-bit at >180 MSPS. At 256 kSPS (0.256 MSPS), this item falls far below all controlled sampling rates despite its 24-bit resolution.

reasoning: This RF transceiver IC appears to be a general-purpose telecommunications component for wireless infrastructure. Without evidence of controlled parameters like frequency switching time specifications, jamming capabilities, or encryption functions, it is classified as EAR99.

This 300 MHz 32-bit audio DSP does not meet control thresholds in Category 3A001 for microprocessors/DSPs, which typically control high-performance processors, radiation-hardened devices, or those with military applications. Standard commercial audio processors fall under EAR99.

reasoning: This is a standard commercial ISM-band RF transceiver using basic FSK/GFSK modulation without encryption, spread spectrum, or other characteristics controlled under Category 5A001. reasoning: This RF transceiver IC operates in commercial frequency bands (75 MHz-6 GHz) with bandwidth (200 MHz) typical of general-purpose wireless communications components. reasoning: Standard commercial Wi-Fi (802.11b/g/n) and Bluetooth/BLE modules operating at 2.4 GHz without controlled encryption capabilities or special features are not specifically listed on the CCL. This cryptographic IC implements AES-128 symmetric encryption (exceeding 56-bit key length) and elliptic curve asymmetric cryptography for data confidentiality, meeting the control parameters of 5A002.a.1.a for cryptographic equipment.

This 16-bit digital signal controller at 60 MIPS does not meet the control thresholds in 3A001.a.2 for microcontrollers, which require an Adjusted Peak Performance of 0.0004 WT or higher. At 60 MIPS with 16-bit architecture, this is a relatively low-performance commercial controller that falls below CCL control parameters.

Basic Layer-2 Ethernet switches operating at 10/100 Mbps without encryption capabilities or other specially controlled features are not specifically listed on the CCL. Category 5A controls focus on telecommunications equipment with encryption, high-performance routing/switching, or surveillance capabilities, none of which apply to this standard industrial Ethernet switch component.

Standard 10/100 Mbps Ethernet PHY transceivers are mass-market networking components that do not meet any controlled parameters in the CCL. They lack the high-speed switching capabilities, encryption features, or specialized telecommunications functions that would trigger controls under Category 5A, and do not meet the performance thresholds for controlled electronic components under Category 3A.

These are widely available commodity components not specifically listed on the CCL.

This microcontroller contains an integrated hardware cryptographic engine implementing AES, 3DES, and SHA algorithms with a random number generator. Items incorporating cryptographic functionality for information security are controlled under 5A002.a.1, which covers equipment using cryptography employing digital techniques performing cryptographic functions other than authentication or digital signature.

Standard commercial Bluetooth Low Energy modules operating at 2.4 GHz are mass market items that do not meet the technical control thresholds in Category 5 telecommunications ECCNs. The module uses widely available Bluetooth 4.2 protocol without specialized encryption or military capabilities that would trigger CCL controls.

This is a standard commercial optical mirror component with broadband visible spectrum coating (400-750 nm).
